packet_markdown:
# The Baxter building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: the-baxter-1818-n-cherokee-ave
- Address: 1818 N Cherokee Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://thebaxterhollywood.com/
- Management company: SD Property Management
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-22

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (low, evidence count 1)
- Management: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 3)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 1)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 3

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: unknown
- Location noise risk: medium
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official site markets The Baxter as a new 86-unit luxury building with a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking. Source: The Baxter official site (https://thebaxterhollywood.com/?rcstdid=Mg%3D%3D-YRjTEizZPgM%3D). Note: Official site describes a brand new 86-unit, 7-story building with rooftop deck, social lounge, and parking.
- [review] Apartments.com lists secured parcel room, rooftop communal workspace, EV chargers, package service, and in-unit washer/dryer. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-baxter-los-angeles-ca/mtsg17j/). Note: Current listing shows secured parcel room, rooftop working space, EV charging, and in-unit washer/dryer.
- [news] Urbanize LA reported the project completed in 2024 with 86 units over a two-level garage, which reinforces the new-building context and thin operating history. Source: Urbanize LA (https://la.urbanize.city/post/rendering-vs-reality-baxter-hollywood-apartments-1818-n-cherokee-avenue). Note: Urbanize LA described a just-completed seven-story building with 86 units and a 61-car garage.

## Open questions
- Because the building is new and review-light, verify noise transfer, package handling, exact unit readiness, concessions, and after-hours support in person. Confirm access control, package handling, and operating maturity under current team.

## Notes
Still worth touring. The amenity package is unusually complete for the target, but nearly all uncertainty is maturity risk: the building is new enough that operating quality, noise transfer, and staff responsiveness still need onsite validation.
